_**User (2026-03-11 21:25:58Z)**_

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested several improvements to the cv-builder web application:
   - Create a `start.sh` script to restart the web app with correct arguments
   - Fix session deletion bug ("Session not found" error)
   - Install `pytest-cov` for test coverage
   - Fix "Analyze Job" errors (JSON parse error, text.replace error, raw JSON display)
   - Fix post-analysis questions being re-asked when already answered
   - Ensure all LLM tasks have access to the full conversation history and all created artifacts (questions, answers, etc.)

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Flask web application with single-session architecture
   - Conversation state machine (phases: init → job_analysis → customization → rewrite_review → generation → refinement)
   - LLM provider abstraction (`LLMClient` ABC with multiple provider implementations)
   - `ConversationManager` with `conversation_history` list and `_build_system_prompt()`
   - `CVOrchestrator` for content selection and document generation
   - Session file-backed state (`session.json` files)
   - Post-analysis questions/answers stored in session state
   - `_strip_context_from_history()` for stripping `context_data` before sending to LLM
   - conda environment management with `conda run`

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`start.sh`** (created)
     - Restart script that kills any process on port 5001, then launches the app via `conda run`
     ```bash
     #!/usr/bin/env zsh
     set -e
     cd "$(dirname "$0")"
     existing=$(lsof -ti tcp:5001 2>/dev/null || true)
     if [[ -n "$existing" ]]; then
       echo "Stopping process on port 5001 (PID $existing)..."
       kill $existing
       sleep 1
     fi
     exec conda run -n cvgen --no-capture-output python scripts/web_app.py --llm-provider 'github' "$@"
     ```

   - **`scripts/web_app.py`**
     - Fixed session deletion: changed `job_dir.parent.resolve() == output_base.resolve()` to `job_dir.resolve().is_relative_to(output_base.resolve())` (line ~1059)
     - Fixed `analyze_job` missing job description: changed return string to `raise ValueError("No job description provided")`
     - Fixed post-analysis questions: removed "cached" early return; now passes `prior_qa` to LLM
     - Updated `_generate_post_analysis_questions` to accept `prior_qa` parameter and inject "Previously answered questions" section into prompt
     - Updated `/api/post-analysis-questions` endpoint to pass `prior_qa=conversation.state.get("post_analysis_answers")`
     - Updated `/api/rewrites` endpoint (pending - not yet done): needs to pass `conversation.conversation_history` and `user_preferences`

   - **`web/app.js`**
     - Fixed delete button to pass `item.path` instead of `item.session_id || item.label`
     - Fixed `deleteSession` to send `{ path: sessionId }` instead of `{ session_id: sessionId }`
     - Fixed `appendMessage` to handle non-string input: `const textStr = typeof text === 'string' ? text : JSON.stringify(text, null, 2)`
     - Fixed `optionsMatch` to use `textStr` instead of `text`
     - Fixed `analyzeJob` to handle all result shapes (string, `{text, context_data}` dict, raw object)
     - Reverted "cached" source shortcut for post-analysis questions

   - **`scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py`**
     - Changed `return "❌ No job description provided"` to `raise ValueError("No job description provided")`
     - Fixed contextual questions messages to include full conversation history:
     ```python
     messages = (
         [{'role': 'system', 'content': system_msg}]
         + self._strip_context_from_history(self.conversation_history)
         + [{'role': 'user', 'content': contextual_prompt}]
     )
     ```
     - Recommend fallback path (line 610): still needs `conversation_history=self.conversation_history` added

   - **`scripts/utils/llm_client.py`**
     - Updated abstract `propose_rewrites` to add `conversation_history` and `user_preferences` optional params
     - Updated `_propose_rewrites_via_chat` to accept and inject those params into the prompt
     - Updated all 5 provider implementations of `propose_rewrites` to pass through the new params
     - Removed `[-10:]` history truncation in `recommend_customizations`; increased per-message char limit from 300 to 800

   - **`scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py`**
     - Updated `propose_rewrites` method to accept and thread `conversation_history` and `user_preferences` through to `self.llm.propose_rewrites()`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`conda activate` fails in non-interactive shell**: Fixed by switching from `conda activate cvgen` to `exec conda run -n cvgen --no-capture-output ...`
   - **"Address already in use" on port 5001**: Fixed by switching from `pgrep -f "python scripts/web_app.py"` to `lsof -ti tcp:5001` to find and kill the process by port
   - **"Session not found" deletion error**: Frontend was passing `item.label` (e.g. "Senior Data Scientist at Acme AI") as session ID; backend needed the full path. Fixed by passing `item.path` from frontend and loosening backend safety check from direct-child-only to `is_relative_to()`
   - **"Analysis Error: Unexpected token '❌'" / JSON parse error**: `_execute_action` returned a plain error string which got wrapped as `{ok: True, result: "❌ ..."}`. Fixed by raising `ValueError` instead so the exception handler returns `{error: ...}`
   - **"text.replace is not a function"**: `appendMessage` called `.replace()` directly on `text` which could be a non-string object. Fixed by coercing: `const textStr = typeof text === 'string' ? text : JSON.stringify(text, null, 2)`
   - **Raw JSON displayed instead of analysis**: `appendFormattedAnalysis` fell through when result was `{text, context_data}` dict. Fixed by handling all result shapes in `analyzeJob` before calling `appendFormattedAnalysis`
   - **Post-analysis questions re-asked on session reload**: Initially tried a "cached" early-return approach, but user corrected: LLM should be called with full history including prior Q&A so it can ask only additional questions. Fixed by passing `prior_qa` to `_generate_post_analysis_questions` and injecting "Previously answered questions" section into LLM prompt
   - **pytest-cov not installed**: Fixed with `conda run -n cvgen pip install pytest-cov`

5. Problem Solving:
   - Session deletion now works reliably using full `session.json` path
   - Analyze Job now correctly raises errors as proper JSON error responses
   - Post-analysis questions now use full conversation context and avoid repeating answered questions
   - All LLM calls now have access to full conversation history (removing truncation) and user preferences

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The issue is clear. The job description itself could be very large, plus the conversation history with Q&A is getting too long. I need to add token-aware truncation.

The best approach:
1. Add a `_truncate_history_to_token_budget` method that estimates tokens and drops old messages when needed

  1788→    def _post(self, payload: dict) -> dict:
  1789→        """POST to Copilot chat completions; refreshes token automatically."""
  1790→        import requests as _req
  1791→        token = self._auth.get_copilot_token()
  1792→        resp = _req.post(
  1793→            "https://api.githubcopilot.com/chat/completions",
  1794→            json=payload,
  1795→            headers={
  1796→                "Authorization":       f"Bearer {token}",

  1800→                "editor-plugin-version": "copilot/1.138.0",
  1801→                "openai-intent":       "conversation-panel",
  1802→                "copilot-integration-id": "vscode-chat",
  1803→            },
  1804→            timeout=120,
  1805→        )
  1806→        if resp.status_code == 401:
  1807→            # Token may have expired mid-flight — force refresh and retry once
  1808→            self._auth._cache.pop("copilot_token", None)
  1809→            self._auth._cache.pop("copilot_expires_at", None)

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-11 21:35:44Z)**_

Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of what was changed:

**Immediate fix** — `config.yaml`: default model changed from `gpt-4o` (8K token limit) to `claude-3.5-sonnet` (200K context).

**Backend** — `web_app.py`:
- `GET /api/model` — returns current provider, model, and list of available models
- `POST /api/model` — switches model at runtime (re-creates `llm_client`, updates `orchestrator.llm` and `conversation.llm`)
- `/api/model` added to lock-exempt paths

**Backend** — `llm_client.py`:
- Added `PROVIDER_MODELS` dict mapping each provider to its available model list

**Frontend** — `web/index.html`:
- Added model `<select>` dropdown in the header, to the left of the auth badge

**Frontend** — `web/ui-core.js`:
- `loadModelSelector()` — fetches available models on page load and populates the dropdown
- `setModel(model)` — POSTs to `/api/model` when the user changes the selection

Restart the app with `./start.sh` to pick up the new default model. The dropdown will show all models supported by `copilot-oauth` (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, claude-3.5-sonnet, claude-3-7-sonnet, o1-preview, o1-mini).
